note: January at Auroville: 20-30°C, dry and clear, the peak window for Matrimandir visits and open-air meditation. Book Matrimandir passes a day ahead — slots go fast.
January at Bekal: 22-32°C, dry trade winds off the Arabian Sea, long afternoons on the fort ramparts. Best month for sunset photos from the bastion.
January at Beypore: 22-32°C, calm sea, active dhow shipyard. Dry weather means Uru builders work full shifts — visit mid-morning for the best yard access.
January at Chennai: 20-29°C, the Margazhi music season in full flow. Cool mornings at Marina Beach, sabha-hopping by night for Carnatic concerts.
January at Cherai: 22-32°C, calm backwaters and sea, dolphins common at dawn off the Chinese nets. Peak houseboat season — weekends book out.
January at Chidambaram: 21-30°C, cool temple mornings at Nataraja, Margazhi devotional singing inside the shrine. Comfortable for the 9-gopuram walk.
January at Dhanushkodi: 22-30°C, calm seas, clear skies over the ghost town ruins. Best month for the jeep ride to land's end — dry track, no cyclone risk.
January at Dholavira: 8-25°C, cold desert nights and warm bright days over the Harappan ruins. Peak Rann season — combine with the nearby white desert.
January at Girnar: 10-26°C, cold pre-dawn starts for the 10,000-step climb, crisp peak views by sunrise. Ropeway runs daily — the best trekking month.
January at Guruvayur: 22-32°C, dry and pleasant for dawn darshan queues. Elephant feeding at Punnathur Kotta stays active; cooler mornings for the long wait.
January at Idukki: 14-26°C, dry air in the cardamom hills, arch dam reservoir at full view. Plantation walks comfortable; leeches gone after the monsoon.
January at Junagadh: 10-26°C, cool days for Uparkot Fort, Buddhist caves, and the Ashokan edicts. Dry trail up Girnar if the ropeway queue runs long.
January at Kanchipuram: 20-30°C, dry and temperate for long temple days across the 1,000-shrine town. Silk weaving sheds open; a good shopping month.
January at Kannur: 22-32°C, peak Theyyam ritual season runs through the Malabar villages. Drive-in Muzhappilangad Beach is at its hardest-packed sand.
January at Kollam: 22-32°C, dry lake weather on Ashtamudi. The 8-hour Kollam-Alleppey cruise runs daily; book the DTPC ferry a day ahead.
January at Kovalam: 22-32°C, calm sea, strong winter sun, lighthouse climb at dusk. Peak season — cliff-edge shacks and Ayurveda retreats book out weeks ahead.
January at Kozhikode: 22-32°C, dry trade-wind weather on the Malabar coast. Beach evenings for biryani runs through Kuttichira; cool enough to walk the old quarter.
January at Kumarakom: 22-32°C, peak Siberian bird migration at Vembanad, dry houseboat cruising. Weekend overnight boats book out — aim for a weekday.
January at Kumbakonam: 20-30°C, cool temple-trail weather across 188 shrines and the Airavatesvara UNESCO site. Degree filter coffee stays hot in the morning chill.
January at Kumbalangi: 22-32°C, dry backwaters, crab-catching tours active, Chinese nets working at dawn. Village homestays run at full capacity.
January at Kuttanad: 22-32°C, below-sea-level paddy fields drained and green, kettuvallam cruises steady. Best month to cycle the dyke roads — no rain, firm track.
January at Lothal: 10-26°C, cool dry weather over the 4,500-year-old Indus dockyard. Morning light shows the brick-lined basin clearly; carry water, no shade on site.
January at Mandvi: 12-27°C, dry sea breeze, dhow shipyard active on the shore, Vijay Vilas Palace open daily. Clear enough to combine with the Kutch Rann.
January at Marari: 22-32°C, calm Arabian Sea, fishing-village mornings with nets coming in at sunrise. Peak season for beach-resort stays — book ahead.
January at Modhera: 10-26°C, cool clear light on the carved Sun Temple, Modhera Dance Festival typically in the third week. Ideal sunrise photography month.
January at Munnar: 5-21°C, cold dry mornings over the tea estates, frost possible in the high valleys. Eravikulam open for Nilgiri tahr — gates get busy by 9am.
January at Nelliyampathy: 10-24°C, dry plantation weather, clear Palakkad plains view from Seetharkund. Ghat road fine after the monsoon — self-drive comfortable.
January at Palitana: 12-28°C, cool pre-dawn starts for the 3,800-step climb to the 863 Jain temples. Dry trail, clear peak views — the year's best trek month.
January at Point Calimere: 22-30°C, peak flamingo congregation on the salt pans, blackbuck out in the open grassland. Morning jeep rides are the best window.
January at Ponmudi: 14-24°C, cool dry hills, butterfly trails still active on sunny afternoons. The 22 hairpins drive fine — road clear of monsoon landslips.
January at Poovar: 22-32°C, calm estuary and sea, dry sandbar clearly exposed. Boat-only island resorts at peak occupancy — weekdays cheaper than weekends.
January at Porbandar: 12-28°C, dry coastal weather at Gandhi's birthplace. Kirti Mandir and Sudama Setu comfortable on foot; Bird Sanctuary lake at good water levels.
January at Rameswaram: 22-30°C, calm sea, dry skies over Pamban Bridge, strong pilgrim season at the temple. Dhanushkodi jeeps run all day from the temple side.
January at Sabarimala: 10-22°C at the shrine, peak Mandala-Makaravilakku season with millions of pilgrims. The Makaravilakku day draws the heaviest crowds.
January at Sasan Gir: 10-28°C, cool dry safari mornings, lions at waterholes as the grass thins. Forest dept jeep slots book out — permits go a week ahead.
January at Srirangam: 20-30°C, dry temple-city weather across the 156-acre complex. Vaikunta Ekadasi in January opens the Paramapada Vasal — heaviest footfall day.
January at Statue of Unity: 10-26°C, clear Narmada Valley views from the 153m gallery. Dry enough for the Valley of Flowers walk; jungle safari park open daily.
January at Talasari: 16-28°C, dry river-mouth sand, calm sea, near-empty beach most weekdays. Shoulder-season weather — the quietest coastal window in Odisha.
January at Tharangambadi: 22-30°C, dry sea-breeze walks along the Danish fort wall and the 1706 Zion Church. Calm surf, clear light — strong photography month.
January at Thekkady: 12-24°C, cool dry spice-plantation weather, elephants at Periyar Lake in the mornings. Boat safaris run every slot — book at the KTDC counter.
January at Trichy: 20-30°C, cool temple-city weather for the 83m Rock Fort climb and Srirangam day-trip. Dry skies, strong evening light on the gopurams.
January at Thiruvananthapuram: 22-32°C, dry winter, peak season for Padmanabhaswamy darshan queues. Museum complex and zoo comfortable on foot through the day.
January at Vagamon: 10-22°C, cold dry meadows and pine forests, paragliding season in full flow on clear mornings. Weekend drives from Kochi fill up fast.
January at Varkala: 22-32°C, calm Arabian Sea below the cliff, strong yoga-retreat season along the north cliff. Peak tourism — cliff-edge cafes run at full capacity.
January at Vedanthangal: 20-30°C, peak nesting season with painted storks, openbills, and pelicans on the tank. Early morning gives the best viewing from the watchtower.
January at Velavadar: 10-28°C, cool grassland safaris with blackbuck in full herd, wolves occasional at dawn. Dry tracks — jeep routes all open through the park.
January at Wayanad: 12-26°C, cool dry Western Ghats weather, Muthanga and Tholpetty safaris active, Edakkal Caves comfortable on the climb. Leech-free trail month.
March at Anamalai: 18-30°C, dry Topslip forest, lion-tailed macaques easier to spot at water sources. Bamboo rafting on the Aliyar runs — forest dept slots book ahead.
March at Mudumalai: 20-34°C, dry Nilgiri biosphere, elephants and gaur at waterholes as the scrub thins. Forest dept safaris run morning and afternoon — morning is cooler.
July at Athirapally: 22-28°C, peak southwest monsoon, the falls at full Niagara-scale volume over the 80ft drop. Spray zone slippery — viewing decks stay open, steps close.
July at Courtallam: 22-30°C, peak monsoon flow through the 9 herb-infused waterfalls, the therapeutic bathing season in full swing. Main falls crowded on weekends.
August at Athirapally: 22-28°C, heavy monsoon flow continues, the falls thundering and the forest misted. Viewing decks open; river crossings and lower trails stay closed.
August at Courtallam: 22-30°C, second monsoon peak feeding all 9 cascades, Saral festival bathing runs through the month. Main Falls gets the thickest weekend crowds.
September at Athirapally: 22-29°C, tapering monsoon, the falls still at strong volume with clearer spray gaps for photos. Forest trails slowly reopen as the track dries.
September at Courtallam: 22-30°C, monsoon easing but flow still strong across the 9 waterfalls, herb-infused bathing still in season. Weekends stay packed; weekdays quieter.
September at Hogenakkal: 24-32°C, Cauvery at strong post-monsoon flow, coracle rides through the cascades at their best. Oil massage stalls on the riverbank run all day.
